g.text: prohibited apparel worn contrary to act
cloth not exceeding 2 shillings per broad yard
any apparel specifically licensed by the king
apparel according to ecclesiastical constitution or equivalent knight/esquire rank
apparel as ordained for yeomen of lords
apparel equivalent to esquires and gentlemen with £100 yearly land/rent
apparel equivalent to esquires and gentlemen with £200 yearly land/rent
apparel exceeding clerical or income-equivalent rank
apparel of stones prohibited except limited headwear context
apparel on body or horses contrary to the act
apparel otherwise prohibited if licensed in writing
apparel used by them or predecessors in occupying/exercising offices
apparel used by virtue of office on body
apparel worn during tenure of office
array equivalent to £40 esquires and gentlemen
cloth above 2 shillings per broad yard
cloth made and supplied according to statutory prices
cloth up to 2s 4d per broad yard
cloth up to 2s per broad yard
clothing not exceeding husband’s permitted level
earlier ordinances and statutes of array or apparel
foreign fur restricted below gentlemanly or qualified status
foreign furs permitted only to qualified groups
foreign furs whose like does not grow within the realm or obedience
foreign furs whose like kind does not grow in england, ireland, wales, or lands under the king’s obedience
fur garments purchased after michaelmas by lower-status persons
fur in winter and lining in summer according to permitted rank
garment cloth above 3s 4d per broad yard
keeping or retaining a servant who has offended against the apparel act
or embroidered with gold or silver for body
silk cap; hat; bonnet; coif; shirt
stuff made outside england, ireland, wales, calais, berwick, or marches, for body or horse apparel
this act supersedes earlier apparel statutes
traditional apparel historically worn by officeholders
